highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.166: Fallback Models & Glob Deny Rules
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.166 introduces a `fallbackModel` setting that lets you configure up to three backup models tried in sequence when your primary model is overloaded or unavailable — great for uninterrupted workflows. It also adds glob pattern support in deny rules, so you can use `'*'` to block all tools at once for tighter security control.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.163: Version Enforcement & Plugin Management
highlights.summary: This release adds `requiredMinimumVersion` and `requiredMaximumVersion` managed settings, allowing teams to enforce which Claude Code versions are permitted — Claude Code will refuse to start outside the allowed range. A new `/plugin list` command (with `--enabled`/`--disabled` filters) makes managing installed plugins much easier for larger setups.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.8, the latest iteration of their most capable model tier. Developers using Claude Code can expect improved reasoning and coding performance, making it worth updating your model configuration to take advantage of the new release.

highlights.title: Show HN: Lessons Learned from Running Claude Code Swarms at Scale
highlights.summary: A developer shares hard-won insights from running large fleets of Claude Code agents in parallel using a Python orchestrator called 'fleet', covering pitfalls and best practices for multi-agent setups. If you're exploring agentic workflows beyond single-session use, this is a practical field report worth reading before you scale up.

highlights.title: Show HN: Boxes.dev — Run Claude Code Agents in the Cloud
highlights.summary: Boxes.dev is a cloud-only agentic dev environment that gives each Claude Code (and Codex) agent its own isolated cloud computer, eliminating the need to run agents on localhost. This is useful if you want to run multiple parallel agents without taxing your local machine or worrying about environment conflicts.

highlights.title: Headroom — Reduce Prompt Bloat to Double Your Claude Code Usage
highlights.summary: Headroom is a macOS menu bar app that silently trims prompt bloat, strips boilerplate, and compresses documents before they reach Claude Code, effectively doubling how much you can do within your existing Claude plan. It works passively in the background without changing your workflow, making it a low-effort way to stretch your token budget.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This compact YouTube tutorial covers Claude Code from the ground up through advanced usage in just 20 minutes, making it ideal if you're short on time but want a solid end-to-end overview. It's a good starting point before diving into longer courses or official documentation.

highlights.title: 9-Minute Claude Code Setup Guide for China Users (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers a concise 9-minute walkthrough covering Claude Code installation, core concepts, and real-world usage — specifically tailored for developers in China who need direct-connection workarounds. If you or your team are based in China and struggling with access, this beginner-friendly guide addresses those friction points head-on.

tips_of_the_day: Configure the new `fallbackModel` setting in your Claude Code settings with at least one backup model (e.g., claude-sonnet) so your agentic tasks continue automatically instead of halting when the primary model is overloaded during peak hours.

Before starting a long multi-file refactor session, add a deny-all glob rule (`"*"`) to your Claude Code permissions for sensitive tool categories, then selectively re-enable only the tools you need — this limits blast radius if the agent goes off-script.

If you're hitting token limits mid-session, try breaking your CLAUDE.md or system prompt into smaller, task-specific includes rather than one large file — leaner context means more room for actual code changes within your plan's usage limits.
